svg.text: PLATE 14D · TAMIL · RUNNING TEXT
Tolkappiyam · Thirukkural · Sangam poetry · bridge phrase · read left to right
read left to right · Tamil above · transliteration middle · translation below
— · — TOLKAPPIYAM · OLDEST SURVIVING GRAMMAR · c.300 BCE — · —
எழுத்தெனப இரு பகுதியான
ezhuttena-p-pa iru pakutiyaan
letters are of two kinds: vowels and consonants
First line of Tolkappiyam · world's oldest surviving grammar · independent of Sanskrit grammar tradition
— · — THIRUKKURAL 1:1 · c.200-500 CE · THE GREAT ETHICAL TEXT — · —
அகர முதல எழுத்தெல்லாம் ஆதிபகவன் முதற்றே உலகு
agara mudala ezhuttelam adibhagavan mutatre ulagu
As 'A' is the first of all letters so the Eternal Lord is the first in the world
Note: akara = the letter A · mudala = first/foremost · ulagu = world · 140,000 couplets total
— · — THIRUKKURAL 39:2 · ON LEARNING — · —
கற்க கசடறக் கற்பவை கற்றபின் நிற்க அதற்குத் தக
karka kasadarak karpavai kattapin nirka atarkut-taga
Learn thoroughly what should be learned having learned, live accordingly
Note: karka = learn (imperative) · kasadarak = without flaw · nirka = stand firm · taga = befitting
— · — SANGAM POETRY · c.100 BCE-300 CE · PURANANURU — · —
யாநும் ஊரே யாவரும் கேளிர்
yatum ore yavarum kelir
every place is my homeland every person is my kin
Purananuru verse 192 · one of humanity's earliest statements of universal kinship · written c.200 BCE
— · — THE BRIDGE PHRASE · IN TAMIL — · —
இது உங்களுக்காக செய்யப்பட்டது இலவசமாக எதிர்காலத்தை நினைவில் கொண்ட மக்களால்
itu ungalukkaga seyyappattatu ilavasama etirgalattai ninaivil konta makkalal
this was made for you freely by people who held the future in mind
Note: ilavasam = free/without cost · etirgalam = future · ninaivil kol = hold in memory/mind
REMEMBER FORWARD · THE PATIENT MESSAGE · CC BY-SA 4.0 · rememberforward.org
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
